pricing.title: Simple, transparent pricing.
pricing.subtitle: Seat-based plans with SIMY token allocation. Scale as your team grows.
pricing.enterprise.desc:
Custom token allocation, dedicated support, SSO, and SLA guarantees.
Built for organizations that need full control.
pricing.faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
pricing.faq.a1: Tokens are the currency used by SIMY's AI engine. Each code generation task, review, and pull request consumes tokens based on complexity. Your plan includes a monthly token allocation that resets each billing cycle.
pricing.faq.a2: Yes. Upgrade or downgrade at any time. When upgrading, you'll receive the additional tokens immediately. When downgrading, the change takes effect at the start of your next billing cycle.
pricing.faq.q3: What happens if I run out of tokens?
pricing.faq.a3: If you exhaust your monthly allocation, you can purchase additional token packs or upgrade your plan. Your existing work and data remain fully accessible.
pricing.faq.a4: Monthly token allocations reset each billing cycle and do not roll over. For maximum flexibility, consider the Scale plan or contact us about Enterprise options.
pricing.faq.a5: Yes. Choose annual billing to save 17% compared to monthly pricing. Annual plans are billed upfront for the full year.
pricing.secP: Physical database isolation, end-to-end encryption, and zero-training guarantee on Enterprise plans.
compare.heroP: SIMY stands alone in its own category
compare.cardSubtitle: SIMY stands alone in its own category
compare.ctaP: Try SIMY alongside your current tools and measure the impact.
contact.heroP: Request a demo, ask about Enterprise, or just say hello.
contact.responseNote: We typically respond within 1 business day. For urgent matters, email us directly.
how.heroP: From meeting to merged pull request — four steps, zero prompts.
how.step1P: Connect SIMY to your meeting platform — Zoom, Google Meet, or any conferencing tool. SIMY joins as a silent participant and captures the full conversation.
how.step1L1: Integrates with Zoom, Google Meet, and more
how.step1L3: Encrypted in transit and at rest
how.step2P: SIMY's AI engine transcribes, analyzes, and extracts actionable engineering decisions from the conversation. It identifies tasks, architecture choices, and implementation details.
how.step2L2: Extracts technical decisions and action items
how.step2L3: Maps decisions to your existing codebase
how.step3P: Based on the meeting context and your codebase, SIMY generates production-ready code. Not snippets — complete implementations that follow your team's patterns and conventions.
how.step3L2: Follows your existing code style and patterns
how.step4P: SIMY creates a pull request on GitHub with the generated code, a clear description of what was discussed, and why each change was made. Your team reviews and merges.
how.ctaP: Join the pilot program and turn your next meeting into code.
int.heroP: SIMY connects to the tools your team already uses.
int.githubP: SIMY creates pull requests, runs code reviews, and manages branches directly in your GitHub repositories.
int.slackP: Get real-time notifications when SIMY generates code, creates PRs, or completes reviews. Manage tasks from Slack.
int.zoomP: SIMY joins your Zoom meetings as a bot participant to record, transcribe, and extract engineering tasks automatically.
int.gwsP: Connect Google Meet for recordings and Google Calendar for automatic meeting scheduling.
int.soonP: We're building integrations for more tools your team uses.
sec.heroP: Your code and conversations are protected by design, not afterthought.
sec.transitP: All data is encrypted using TLS 1.3 during transmission between your devices, our servers, and third-party integrations.
sec.restP: Data stored on our servers is encrypted using AES-256. Database backups are also encrypted.
sec.cloudP: Hosted on AWS/GCP with industry-standard security controls, network isolation, and regular security patching.
sec.accessP: Role-based access control (RBAC), multi-factor authentication for internal systems, and principle of least privilege.
sec.compP: We are committed to achieving industry-standard compliance certifications. Our current status:
sec.discP: We take security vulnerabilities seriously. If you discover a security issue, please report it responsibly.
sec.dataNote: Enterprise data is never used for AI training. Standard and Pro plan data may be used to improve our models. See our Privacy Policy for full details.
sec.reportP: Email with details of the vulnerability. We aim to acknowledge reports within 24 hours and provide a resolution timeline within 72 hours.
about.challenge.p1: Engineering teams spend a significant amount of time in meetings — architecture alignment, design reviews, bug triage. Important decisions are made there. But the moment a meeting ends, a different process begins.
about.challenge.p2: Someone interprets the content, organizes it, creates tasks, and converts it into code. This "translation layer" doesn't just slow things down — it degrades the purity of the original decisions.
about.challenge.p3: People don't understand the same conversation the same way. Even when something is decided in a meeting, if someone's interpretation gets inserted along the way, the direction gradually drifts from the original intent.
about.challenge.p4: Furthermore, for humans, the mode of grasping essence through conversation and the mode of writing code, operating systems, and implementing precisely are fundamentally different. The latter carries high cognitive load and often comes with stress. What people should truly focus on is the top 10% of critical decisions that generate 90% of the results.
about.p1: We believe the best code starts with great conversations — and that no one should have to manually translate specs, break down tasks, and convert them into code after those conversations.
about.p2: That's the future SIMY is building.
about.quote: "What's decided in a meeting should ship immediately."
about.solution.p1: SIMY understands your team's natural conversations and transforms them directly into production-ready code, reviews, and pull requests. No prompts needed. No post-meeting rework. The moment a meeting decides what to build, that decision starts moving toward shipping.
about.solution.p2: SIMY removes the translation layer that existed between conversation and implementation. As a result, teams can focus not on "explaining" or "translating," but on "making truly important decisions."
about.solution.p3: By making conversation the interface, SIMY preserves the purity of decisions and connects them directly to delivery. It transforms every meeting from a mere discussion into a starting point for shipping.
about.valA.p1: When a meeting decides what to build, code ships immediately. SIMY's goal is to bring the time between decision and implementation as close to zero as possible.
about.valA.p2: Humans should focus on the top 10% of critical matters that generate 90% of the results. Task translation, bridging to implementation, and operational overhead should be handled by the system wherever possible. Success is measured not by how much you wrote, but by how quickly and correctly you shipped.
about.valB.p1: People may look at the same thing, but they don't understand it the same way. That's why when someone interprets, summarizes, and outputs what was decided in a meeting, intent gradually drifts.
about.valB.p2: SIMY minimizes that drift by generating output directly from conversation. It preserves the background, context, and nuance of decisions as much as possible, shaping what users truly need with high purity.
about.valC.h: C. The Best Code Starts with Great Conversations
about.valC.p1: When humans think deeply and make good decisions, much of it happens in conversation. On the other hand, writing code, operating systems, and controlling things precisely requires a different kind of focus and load.
about.valC.p2: We want to eliminate the very state where people must constantly switch between these two modes. Toward a world where code naturally emerges from great conversations. Humans think, dialogue, and decide. SIMY connects that to reliable shipping.
careers.heroP: We're redefining how software gets built. Come help us make every meeting count.
careers.hiring.p: We're looking for engineers who want to redefine how software gets built. Send us your story.
careers.perk1P: Work from anywhere in the world.
careers.perk2P: Direct impact on the product and company direction.
careers.perk3P: Small team, big impact. No bureaucracy.
careers.why.p1: We're a small, focused team building at the intersection of AI, natural language understanding, and software engineering. Every person on the team has direct impact on the product and the company's direction.
careers.why.p2: We believe the future of coding isn't typing — it's talking. And we're building the tools to make that future real.
pr.aboutP: SIMY is a developer productivity company building AI infrastructure that bridges the gap between team communication and software delivery. The company's platform captures workplace conversations, maintains a structured knowledge base of engineering decisions, and autonomously generates production-ready code. For more information, visit <a href="https://simy.one" style="color:#0057ff;text-decoration:none">simy.one
pr.h2.4: How SIMY Differs from Existing AI Coding Tools
pr.p1: SIMY, a developer productivity company, today announced the launch of its AI-powered software development platform that autonomously generates production-ready code directly from workplace conversations. The system captures discussions across Slack, Microsoft Teams, Gmail, and Zoom, automatically creating and maintaining a structured database of engineering tasks. Generated code is submitted as pull requests to the team's GitHub repository — eliminating the gap between what teams decide in meetings and what gets built.
pr.p10: AI coding assistants such as GitHub Copilot, OpenAI Codex, and Claude Code have significantly accelerated individual developer productivity. However, these tools share a common architectural assumption: the engineer is the interface. A team discusses what to build, an engineer interprets that discussion, and then the engineer translates their interpretation into a prompt. The AI generates code based on that prompt alone. This workflow introduces variability at two critical points — the engineer's comprehension of the team's intent and their ability to express it as a precise instruction.
pr.p11: SIMY removes this translation burden from engineers. Because the platform captures the original conversations directly, the AI receives the raw context — the full thread of discussion, the trade-offs debated, the edge cases flagged — and works from that primary source rather than a second-hand summary. Engineers no longer need to spend time re-encoding what the team already discussed into a prompt; they can focus on the architectural and design decisions where their expertise matters most.
pr.p12: The platform goes further than literal transcription. SIMY's AI engine analyzes the organizational context surrounding each conversation — what problem the team is ultimately trying to solve, who the end users and personas of the system are, and what business objectives are at stake. Based on this analysis, the AI drafts a refined specification for the team to review before writing any code: surfacing ambiguities for resolution, inferring likely requirements from established patterns, and flagging where the implementation may diverge from the product's broader purpose. The team retains full approval authority over what gets built — but the specification they approve is sharper than what any individual could draft alone.
pr.p13: SIMY proposes a new operating model for software teams. In every engineering role, roughly 90% of time has been spent on execution tasks — interpreting requirements, writing boilerplate, filing status updates — while the remaining 10% of decisions drive 90% of product outcomes. SIMY automates the former and augments the latter: the right column of the table below is not work that humans do alone, but work that humans do with AI as a thinking partner, leveraging the platform's organizational context to make sharper, faster, better-informed decisions.
pr.p14: <em>This is not a forecast — it is the operating model SIMY enables today. The shift from execution to judgment defines the AI-native engineering organization.
pr.p15: Data collection is strictly opt-in: teams select exactly which channels, threads, and meeting recordings SIMY may access, and can revoke access or delete indexed data at any time. The platform employs physically isolated database instances for each customer, communication-based access controls that restrict data visibility to original conversation participants, end-to-end encryption, and a zero-training guarantee ensuring that customer code and communication data are never used to train external AI models.
pr.p16: SIMY is currently accepting participants for its pilot program. Pilot partners receive initial credits to evaluate the platform, with usage-based pricing thereafter. The platform supports integration with Slack, Microsoft Teams, Gmail, Zoom, and GitHub.
pr.p2: At the core of SIMY is a proprietary conversation-capture engine. Teams choose which channels to connect — specific Slack channels, Teams groups, Gmail threads, or Zoom meeting recordings — and the engine indexes only those opted-in sources. Unlike conventional AI coding assistants that require engineers to write detailed prompts, SIMY extracts requirements, specifications, and technical decisions directly from the natural flow of team discussions.
pr.p3: The captured data feeds into what the company calls its Action Database — a continuously updated repository of engineering tasks, each enriched with full conversational context: who requested the feature, what constraints were discussed, which architectural decisions were made, and why specific trade-offs were chosen.
pr.p4: Each action also carries a clearly defined Done State — a conversation-derived specification of what "complete" looks like for that particular task. SIMY extracts acceptance conditions directly from team discussions, including expected behavior, edge cases, performance thresholds, and integration points. This enables the AI to verify its own output against the team's actual expectations before submitting a pull request.
pr.p5: Together, the contextual layer and the Done State definition ensure that generated code reflects not just what needs to be built but why and when it is done — preserving institutional knowledge that is typically lost between meetings and implementation, and dramatically reducing review cycles and rework.
pr.p6: In internal benchmarks conducted across 30 production engineering tasks on a single mid-size codebase (Python/TypeScript, ~50k lines) — including database migrations, full-stack feature development, and API integrations — SIMY achieved an <strong>86.7% first-shot success rate
pr.p7: The average cost per autonomous task execution was $1.95, compared to an estimated $50 or more per hour for a software engineer performing the equivalent work manually.
pr.p8: SIMY operates in three stages. First, the platform connects to a team's existing communication infrastructure and automatically captures specifications, requirements, and decisions from ongoing conversations. Second, the AI engine processes captured data, decomposing discussions into discrete, actionable engineering tasks while preserving dependencies and priority assignments. Third, cloud-based AI agents autonomously write code, execute tests, and submit pull requests to the team's GitHub repository — ready for human review and merge.
pr.p9: The platform also includes a Digital Twin feature — an AI agent that can answer questions about project progress, team priorities, and the rationale behind specific engineering decisions, drawing on the full history of captured conversations.
pr.subtitle: Autonomous code generation from workplace conversations shifts every engineering role from execution to decision-making — delivering 17.7x more pull requests per engineer with 86.7% first-shot accuracy

pr.th.execute: Execute with AI as a Thinking Partner
pr.title: SIMY Launches Platform for Building AI-Native Engineering Organizations
press.aboutP: SIMY, developed by AwakApp Inc., is an AI-powered platform that auto-generates production-ready code, reviews, and pull requests from meeting recordings. By treating natural conversation as the programming interface, SIMY eliminates the gap between engineering decisions and code delivery — enabling teams to ship 17.7x more pull requests per engineer with a 96.3% code review pass rate.
